page.content: In evaluating the effectiveness and safety of various chemotherapeutic regimens for lung cancer, the associated adverse events play a critical role in guiding treatment decisions. The data presented in the table highlights the prevalence of common adverse events such as nausea, vomiting, nephrotoxicity, and bone marrow suppression across different chemotherapy protocols. Notably, the table includes metrics related to patient responses both at baseline and at the end of the study, allowing for a comprehensive understanding of the impact of treatment over time.

lines.text: In evaluating the effectiveness and safety of various chemotherapeutic regimens for lung cancer, the associated adverse events
play a critical role in guiding treatment decisions. The data presented in the table highlights the prevalence of common adverse
events such as nausea, vomiting, nephrotoxicity, and bone marrow suppression across different chemotherapy protocols.
Notably, the table includes metrics related to patient responses both at baseline and at the end of the study, allowing for a
comprehensive understanding of the impact of treatment over time.

page.html:
Cancer typeChemotherapeutic regimensCommon adverse events
LungPlatinum-basedNausea, vomiting, nephrotoxicity, bone marrow suppression
BreastAdriamycin-based or taxane-basedBone marrow suppression, nausea, vomiting, alopecia
StomachOxaliplatin/5-fluouracilNausea, vomiting, mucositis, diarrhea, bone marrow suppression
LiverAdriamycin and CisplatinNausea, vomiting, bone marrow suppression, nephrotoxicity
ColorectalOxaliplatin-based or Irinotecan-basedNausea, vomiting, diarrhea, bone marrow suppression

page.content: For instance, the mean percentage of 3 H-cholesteryl ester notably decreased from 4.2 ± 0.8 at baseline to 3.5 ± 0.7 at the end of the study, indicating a potential shift in lipid metabolism in response to platinum-based regimens. This information underscores the importance of monitoring these adverse events in patients undergoing treatment, as they can significantly influence quality of life and overall treatment adherence. Such insights are crucial for clinicians when tailoring personalized treatment plans for lung cancer patients.
